revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Ashihara Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ashihara shrine.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, Nakamura-cho, Mukogaichi 813[http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area09.php "125 Shrines 5" - Shikinen Sengu Memorial Sengukan, accessed May 3, 2015] |Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 28 | Latitude Seconds = 20.9 |Longitude Degrees = 136 | Longitude Minutes = 43 | Longitude Seconds = 43.8 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Sasatsu-hiko no Mikoto
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇加乃御玉御祖命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Ikari-hime no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other Names = Iga-i no Mori |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.472472|N|136.728833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Ashihara Shrine''' (葭原神社, Ashihara-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakamura-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is a subordinate shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It Ranks 3rd among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14. Ashihara Shrine is identified with the "Ogihara Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}.Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 394. It is located within the precincts of {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Naikū)|ja|月讀宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Naikū)|qq=}}, one of the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine. == Overview == {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara Shrine|qq=}} is located near the back approach entrance of {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya|ja|月読宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyomi-no-Miya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 月讀宮}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.106Gakken Publishing (2013): p.62 It is situated at the southern edge of the precinct of Tsukiyomi-no-Miya.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.395 While shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, known as Shikinaisha, are usually auxiliary shrines,Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 Yoshihara Shrine is Ranked as a subsidiary shrine under the Inner Shrine. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} architectural style with a wooden board roof, facing south, and is surrounded by a single {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} (sacred fence).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.397 There is one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=Torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q234731}}. A large {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|qid=Q158722|lt=Camphor tree|1=Camphora officinarum}} grows nearby. No {{ill|Saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saisenbako|qid=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}} (offertory box) is provided. The area of the shrine grounds is approximately 238 square meters. == Shrine Name == The name "Yoshihara" means a reed field, indicating that the area around Yoshihara Shrine was once covered with reeds. The name appears in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" and was categorized as a non-government registered rice field shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, different manuscripts list the name as "{{ill|Ogiwara Shrine|ja|荻原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogiwara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Hagiwara Shrine|ja|萩原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagiwara Shrine|1=Hagiwara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620360}}," but later studies determined "Ogiwara Shrine" as the correct reading.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp.394-395 Locally, it is referred to as "Igai no Mori" (いがいのもり), derived from the enshrined deity's name. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Sasatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|佐佐津比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uka-no-mitama-no-mioya-no-Mikoto|ja|宇加乃御玉御祖命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-mitama-no-mioya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikari-hime no Mikoto|ja|伊加利比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikari-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} All three deities are guardians of rice fields and are worshipped for abundant harvests. Sasatsu-hiko no Mikoto is recorded as the child of {{ill|Toshigami|lt=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|qq=}} in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō." Although "御祖" (Mioya) appears in the name of Uka-no-mitama-no-mioya-no-Mikoto, it is identified as the same deity as {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-mitama|1=Ukanomitama}} in "Daijingū Gishikai."Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.396 "Ikari" in Ikari-hime no Mikoto is thought to refer to "rice harvesting" or something related to fields, as mentioned in "Jingū Tenryaku." == History == The shrine was established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. It appears in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō" (Enryaku Gishikichō), indicating its existence before the compilation of this record in 804. Moreover, the "{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}" dated February 23, 858, includes the following entry: {{Cquote|Located in Ise Province, Ranked as a 6th-grade shrine, Yoshihara Shrine|qq=}} In the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō," it is listed as "Ogiwara Shrine," leading to debates over time about whether Yoshihara Shrine and Ogiwara Shrine were the same. Scholars such as Arakida Tsunemasa and Sonoda Moroyoshi concluded they were the same, pronouncing it "Ogiwara." However, in the Meiji period, the shrine was officially identified as Yoshihara Shrine with the pronunciation "Ashihara." The shrine fell into disrepair during the medieval period, and by the early Meiji era, its location was unknown. Efforts to restore 21 shrines with discontinued buildings began during the early Meiji period.Sakurai (1991): p.281 In 1874, the restoration of shrines like {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}} was completed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Yoshihara Shrine was rebuilt in 1880 using old materials from {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine was reconstructed in March 1918 and again in July 1957. == Access == Located along {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|qid=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 23}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.103 ;Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505724|1=Toba Line}} {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|qid=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} - About 10 minutes on foot (approx. 820 m). * {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} Bus - "Nakamura-cho" bus stop, about 3 minutes on foot (approx. 250 m). ;By Car * {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|qid=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}} {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884857}} - About 1 minute south via National Route 23 (approx. 900 m). Free parking available for Tsukiyomi-no-Miya visitors. == Related Pages == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == References == == References == * Ise Bunka Publishing. Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise. December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office. Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1. January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * Gakken Publishing. Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine. July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine. November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * Shikinaisha Research Association. Shikinaisha Research Report, Volume 6 (Tokaido 1). February 28, 1990, 690 pages. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0308.html 葭原神社（あしはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|葭原神社|version=90499811|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Ashihara Shrine (Q17191305)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|qid=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Ashihara Shrine|lt=Ashihara Shrine|ja|葭原神社|qid=Q17191305|1=Ashihara Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|qid=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.472478|136.728851|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181157 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17191305}} [[da:d:Q17191305]] [[ja:葭原神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]==Notes== [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]]
